model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that starts at a home depot, visits every city exactly once, and returns to the depot. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the trip travels directly from the first to the second, and we track the order in which cities are visited along the route. Each direct leg between two cities carries a known travel cost. The objective is to choose the route that makes the total travel cost over all legs taken as small as possible.

sets.name: I
sets.doc: the cities to be visited on the tour; the first member in order is the depot where the tour starts and ends

params.name: c
params.doc: the travel cost of going directly from the first city to the second city of the pair, indexed over ordered pairs of cities

params.name: n
params.doc: the number of cities, equal to the count of members of the city set; used as the big constant in the ordering constraints

vars.name: x
vars.doc: takes the value one when the tour travels directly from the first city of the pair to the second city of the pair, and zero otherwise; the diagonal self-pairs are fixed to zero

vars.name: p
vars.doc: the position of each city along the tour, ranging from zero up to the number of cities minus one; the depot is fixed at position zero

description: Every city must be departed from exactly once. For each city, exactly one direct leg of the trip leaves that city and heads to some city.

description: Every city must be arrived at exactly once. For each city, exactly one direct leg of the trip enters that city coming from some city.

model_narrative: We plan a single round trip that starts at a home depot, visits every city exactly once, and returns to the depot. For each ordered pair of cities we decide whether the trip travels directly from the first to the second, and we track the order in which cities are visited along the route. vars.name: p

description: To rule out disconnected sub-loops, the visiting positions of the cities must stay consistent with the legs that are actually taken. For each ordered pair of distinct cities that does not involve the depot, whenever the trip travels directly from the first city to the second, the second city must come later in the visiting order than the first. This is enforced with the standard ordering inequality that stays slack when that leg is not taken.
